description_long: This object features a pair of gracefully curved, spiral staircases suspended above a flat rectangular base, with each staircase rising in opposite directions. The staircases are adorned with slender, evenly spaced balusters and a smooth, continuous handrail, giving the piece a sense of elegance and movement. The overall form is highly sculptural, with the staircases appearing to float in midair, connected only by a central axis. The metallic finish of the structure suggests a polished silver or platinum material, lending a refined and modern aesthetic. The design is symmetrical and geometric, emphasizing clean lines and a minimalist approach. There are no visible gemstones or additional embellishments, focusing attention on the architectural motif.
description: A sculptural, spiral staircase-inspired jewelry piece with a polished metallic finish and minimalist, geometric design.
description_view_0: From this view, the twin spiral staircases are seen from the front, highlighting their upward curves and the symmetry of the balusters.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the depth and curvature of the staircases, showing the handrails and the floating effect more clearly.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the back of the staircases, with the balusters and central axis visible, and the base appearing more prominent.
description_view_3: From this angle, the spiral nature and the separation between the two staircases are most apparent, with the handrails forming elegant arcs.
